more.title: How the Skeleton of Mutapa Times is Based on Sarah Charlesworth's Work
more.description: Conceptual artist Sarah Charlesworth's 1979 study of how newspapers covered a solar eclipse inspired the framework behind The Mutapa Times.

more.title: Court hears final evidence in Muvevi murder trial
more.description: Source: Court hears final evidence in Muvevi murder trial – herald Fidelis Munyoro-Chief Court Reporter THE murder trial of former police detective Jaison Muvevi closed with gripping testimonies from two State psychiatrists, who assessed his mental fitness amidst charges of four murders and four attempted murders. more.title: 17 magistrates sworn in
more.description: Source: 17 magistrates sworn in – herald Zvikomborero Parafini-Court Reporter CHIEF Magistrate Vongai Guwuriro yesterday presided over the swearing-in of 17 magistrates, bringing the total number of magistrates in Zimbabwe to 268. more.title: Gvt issues monthly reprieve on basic commodities imports
more.description: Source: Gvt issues monthly reprieve on basic commodities imports – herald Farirai Machivenyika Senior Reporter The Government has exempted individuals from obtaining import licences for various basic commodities once a month, according to Statutory Instrument 59 of 2026, cited as Control of Goods (Import and Export) (Commerce) (Amendment) Regulations, 2026 (No. 15). more.title: Fidelity forced to buy specialised machines as suspected Chinese miners flood market with tungsten infused gold
more.description: GOVERNMENT owned Fidelity Refineries and Printers (FRP) has been forced to purchase specialised machinery to analyse large amounts of gold being delivered after revelations that some named Chinese miners were mixing tungsten and gold to increase purity and weight of their product. The post Fidelity forced to buy specialised machines as suspected Chinese miners flood market with tungsten infused gold appeared first on NewZimbabwe.com .

more.title: Kuimba Shiri claims US$86,000 in damages due to Lake Chivero pollution as court knocks out Harare council, ZINWA defence
more.description: Kuimba Shiri (Private) Limited is claiming US$86,240 in damages against the City of Harare, Environmental Management Agency (EMA), Zimbabwe National Water Authority and the Zimbabwe Parks and Wildlife Management Authority after pollution at Lake Chivero devastated its business. The claim has gained momentum after the High Court struck out opposition from the City of Harare [...] The post Kuimba Shiri claims US$86,000 in damages due to Lake Chivero pollution as court knocks out Harare council, ZINWA defence appeared first on NewZimbabwe.com .

more.title: MDC to stage demonstration against Constitutional Amendment Bill extending Mnangagwa’s term
more.description: MOVEMENT for Democratic Change (MDC) will next week hold a protest in Harare against the Constitutional Amendment Bill Number 3, which seeks to extend the tenure of President Emmerson Mnangagwa beyond 2028. The MDC has since written a letter to the Zimbabwe Republic Police to request permission to protest from Africa Unity Square and in [...] The post MDC to stage demonstration against Constitutional Amendment Bill extending Mnangagwa’s term appeared first on NewZimbabwe.com .

more.title: Woman arrested for child brutality after forcing minor to touch burning gas stove in viral video
more.description: POLICE have arrested a Harare woman captured in a viral video while torturing her son for allegedly stealing from a local tuck shop.
